text:
Title:
Apartment Recommendations

Entry 2:
Hello,
I just graduated and am staying in Pullman. I currently live in the lovely Timberling on the Green (the Ruckus)... lol
I need a good recommendation for a studio (1x1) apartment that is UNFURNISHED with a washer/dryer, dishwasher, good internet, and won't break the bank (less than 850/month). I have some wiggle room but the most important thing for me is in unit washer/dryer and decent internet or the option to buy my own. I would also be interested in renting a room in a 2x2 with the same amenities.
If this exists in the land of Pullman please lmk! I can't seem to find anything nice that offers unfinished rooms (most of the new apartments are furnished and meant for students).

Entry 3:
You'd be paying at least 1k per month at this point

Entry 5:
Pimlico actually has some 1br/1bth for $945/mo
Also has washer + dryer

Entry 6:
Washer+dryer 2 bed at College Crest is 750+utilities

Entry 7:
Is it better to schedule a tour or just go down there & ask about available units?

Entry 8:
I would probably schedule, most of the time the office is out for maintenance, but if you schedule ahead of time you won't skip a beat.

Entry 10:
If you are able to secure a unit at the SummerHill apartments, I know those can cost around $900 per month. I'm going off of someone who lived in a 2 bedroom unit there with an in-unit washer and dryer. Seemed like a great price for all the space and amenities they got. Pimlico might be a good option to check out as well. Good luck!

Entry 11:
under 850 for all that? Keep dreaming.

Entry 12:
What would you say is a better price range?

Entry 15:
Bro, I live there, they changed prices.

Entry 16:
I just saw an ad for prices. Reasonable

Entry 18:
I don't have any, anyone who attracts roaches is just leaving food out and being gross. If price is not that important to you, go ahead and worry about a problem that has probably been fixed with an exterminator anyway? I guarantee every place in town has had mice, bugs, you name it. You deal with those things as you go in apartments and houses

Entry 19:
You didn't prove me wrong, you just moved the goalpost silly

Entry 20:
You definitely work for college crest or something because you're sucking them off harder than a crack head trying to get $5 for their next fix

Entry 21:
Nah, just like the big rooms and think that people are being suckered when they end up with places like the ruckus instead

Entry 22:
The ruckus and college crest are the two worst reviewed complexes in pullman, So why don't you stfu with your broke ass

Entry 23:
Are they your family members 🤔?

Entry 27:
I don't think so, because I went to go look.
I am looking for a room in Pullman also, for me and my service dog.
I got some raw land up further up but no house.
So Pullman would be a good home base until I could build something or get a presence up there as it's rural.
There are some low income places but having a hard time locating them.
Pullman has changed quite a bit since I graduated.

Entry 29:
I don't mind roaches.. They are easy to kill. Had to deal with them in Florida for the intershthat turned into a 20+ career.
I have mice now where I rent Idaho because the logger keeps leaving the house doors open 24/7 because there is no AC in his 1910 house.
My cat recently died, and cats are good for getting rid of mice and roaches, too.
So thanks for the information.. I am going to check into it later today 😀
The pictures that I saw posted that originally turned me away were more of building structure issues.

Entry 30:
You say that when you live there lmao

Entry 31:
I do, but I don't throw hissy fits over little issues. I like the fact that every dollar I don't spend I get to save.

Entry 32:
You're the only one throwing a fit here because people aren't interested in your shitty apartment complex you dumb poor fuck.

Entry 33:
College Crest has exactly what you're both looking for

Entry 35:
I'm not saying it's fantastic; it's far from perfect, but the room size is the biggest possible and the prices are the best you can find. It's like 350 for the 3 bed right now.

Entry 36:
I saw the add but then saw I need proof of the 3x monthly rent, but although I get ssdi now..it will take awhile to get a benefit statement.
So I am probably going " camping. "

Entry 37:
You can always get a guarantor

Entry 39:
just stop dude, you're not even replying to anyone.

Entry 40:
Washer/dryer included will drive the price up. I've only lived in places with on-site laundromats so I can't really give a recommendation on that front

Entry 42:
Those units are for graduate students not students who just graduated.

Entry 44:
I didn't realize you could rent unfinished units at WSU housing.

Entry 46:
What is your honest opinion of the former " Ruckus "

Entry 47:
Hit and miss on maintenance. They were quick on some things and unresponsive on others. The prices are steep for what it offers, but it looks like they’re going down? There is a lot of work to be done here as far as consistency of unit quality and maintenance. I wouldn’t recommend to anyone who can do better!

Entry 48:
Well, someone mentioned College Crest & the reviews with pictures were rather dumpy.
Not a place I'd wa to.
As far as the Ruckas...( Timberline on the Green) the current reviews indicate another management change that actually benefits the
students.

Entry 49:
Reviews have looked the same, poor. But my experience has been fair since the change in management

Entry 50:
im trying to get someone to take over the lease in my bfs coug ridge apt so im biased but i also live in the ruckus and tbh youre dreamin. i was too! which is why im at the ruckus lmao it was just about the best i could do with amenities given the price range. so id stay at the ruckus if i were you or give up w/d (thats what i refused to give up, hence the ruckus). if you do give up w/d then my bfs apt is pretty good for all those LMAO except being $1000. idk any that are lower that arent rooms in larger apts. so ya prob just stay at the ruckus unless youre cool with giving something up

Entry 52:
So is College Crest 🤔 one of those apartments where you just rent by the room?
I am going to schedule a tour, once I find out where the location is. :)

Title:
Best apartments for students off campus?

Entry 2:
Hi! In your opinion, what are the best off-campus apartments for students at WSU? Super curious to hear what yall think

Entry 3:
Not the ruckus

Entry 4:
It depends entirely who lives in your little area. Some are wild, some are just the opposite.

Entry 5:
I wouldn't recommend Aspen Heights either. In fact I'd highly recommend avoiding Aspen altogether. That whole fiasco was such a nightmare and even after move-out we're dealing with issues. Unbelievably poor for what it should have been. There's a reason they're renting for $509/mo right now. Nobody else is willing to.

Entry 6:
Summerhills/Boulder creek. Very cheap but still nice. Also heard good thing about the flats.

Entry 7:
Reaney Park apartments, hands down. They're outdated and they're total crap but they're cheap as shit and they're conveniently close to the park, the public pool and there are stairs that literally lead right to WSU. The kicked out all the psychopaths that used to live there and now it's just grad students and a few families.

Entry 8:
I'm living at Birch Hills right now and so far I'm a fan! WAY better than living at the Ruckus 💀

Entry 9:
I think it really depends on your criteria! Do you value proximity/walkability to campus? To the grocery store? What’s your price range? Roommates? Private vs shared bathroom? Etc

Entry 10:
The Flats 💪

Entry 11:
Anything Dabco is gonna be a little more expensive, but well worth it imo. Great, reliable management and consistently nice apartments

Entry 12:
if you want an apartment near the campus, evolve on main is fully furnished. Not too bad and almost has everything you need. I also have a lease to relet, feel free to contact me if you want to have a tour:)

Title:
Best One Bedroom Apartments

Entry 2:
I’m a new grad student coming to wsu this fall and I was wondering if people had recommendations for the best one bedroom apartments around Pullman. I’m looking to keep it under $1000 a month any and all recommendations or help is appreciated!

Entry 3:
Take a look at any of the Dabco apartments they have several properties in Pullman. The management is decent and most of the locations are close to campus and bus routes.

Entry 4:
I agree with this, birch hills is a good complex too

Entry 5:
Normandy, Wil Ru… I don’t know what they are called but the ones directly to the right when you enter Reaney Park. Those are the ones I’d check out if my current situation fell through.

Entry 6:
I've heard bad things from a lot of people about Timberline so I'd stay away from them.
Apartments.com
might be your best bet to find one right now, but I would try to get one quickly, as a lot of apartments are already claimed for next year.

Entry 7:
Search “WSU off campus housing” and it’ll list all the properties- most are very near the campus. My husband signed a lease for one of the Dabco properties and the one-bedroom is only $875/month. There’s a bus that picks up right next to the apartment and drops off at locations around campus, so you don’t have to pay for a parking pass for campus.

Entry 9:
If you're open to a 1 room studio, 710 Oak Street has decent/responsive management, and rent is 700-600 a month (600 if you do a year long lease iirc). It's small space but the closest you can get to campus while being 'off campus'

Entry 2:
(Disclaimer: if you have a personal vendetta against DABCO for some random reason please don't post here--I need rational, balanced advice, not dramatized stories about how shitty your life is)
It's getting late in the year and most of the apartments that everyone says are good are bought up by now. I'm considering Churchill Downs because it has availability, is cheap, and looks.. fine. However, almost everything I read online (never been to pullman in person b/c covid so idk anyone to ask personally) says DABCO is the scum of the earth, avoid them at all costs, etc. Are they really that bad..? Or are people just over exaggerating? I don't need a perfect apartment, or perfect management, but I also really don't need to be price gouged and scammed either. I'm aware that your apartment must be in tip-top shape to get the deposit back.

But the things I've read make DABCO out to seem like the worst thing on earth. If it's livable and I can be savvy enough to keep good records, take pictures when I arrive, etc., would it really be that bad to live at Churchill?

Entry 3:
Helenes is the scum of Pullman renting. With Dabco you just really gotta make sure you turn in the move in check list ASAP after you get your stuff in and the contract signed, and make sure you keep your copy in a known place for your move out. They
will
try to nickel and dime you for any and all damage after you're out. So you gotta document everything wrong with the apartment when you move in. Take your own pictures and keep them (or the flash drive/SD card whatever) with your copy of the move-in document. If they don't have a document that allows you to look for and track damage, faulty/hinky appliances, bad doors, carpets, windows, etc...then take the time to create a word doc or something that lists all problems and insist they have a copy in your renter's file.

It's a hassle but the renters in Pullman (and any college town) know full well they take advantage of young, first time renters all the time. Don't let them hose you for money...which also means pay your rent on time, mind the parking lot rules and all that.

Entry 4:
This summarizes them as a whole. Dabco is predatory since they can get away with it due to most renters not knowing how to take the proper precautions, but they aren't the worst. I had financial struggles while renting through Dabco ~3 years ago and they did help me with payment plans without backlash.
That being said, learn to protect yourself no matter who you rent through. Follow the above advice, and look up other ways to protect yourself, and Dabco should be fine to rent through.

Entry 5:
Exactly. Dabco is fine if you know your tenants rights and take pictures. I like that the apartments were built more recently than a lot of other places in town. My biggest complaint was that they took forever to clear the snow from the parking lots and sidewalks.

Entry 6:
I found it amazing how fast and great they are in snow removal, amazing, if you ever were in Seattle you'd know this, in Seattle only most traveled roads are cleared never within a complex parking lot, so we're very lucky with snow clearing.

Entry 7:
Almost all of the property management companies in town suck. If you can, go with a private landlord. There are tons of "for rent" signs around town right now.

Entry 8:
Whenever you move in to a place do a walkthrough (twice) with your phone. One video walkthrough that briefly points out any scuffs/damage/carpet issues/appliance issues etc.
Then circle back and take detailed pictures of these items. Save them all to a Cloud location you can access later and won't lose if you lose your phone.
Never lost a dollar of a deposit in any place I've ever rented, these were the first two steps I'd take whenever I move in. One Pullman company did attempt to charge me on moving out for an appliance issue and I had video proof that that was the way it was when I moved in (and I'd requested it fixed multiple times). My deposit was paid the next day.

Entry 9:
I'm over in Providence Court and it's honestly about average for a college apartment. Like any other place in town they're gonna try to squeeze as much as they can but as long as you're smart about documenting and taking pics you'll be fine. Haven't had any issues with the office people either.
Maintenance is pretty quick and the Churchill downs apartments are at the end of the express route as well, and it's about a 20 minute walk to the CUB if you take B street.

Entry 10:
With Dabco, I walked around with my phone and recorded every little speck I found on the initial walk through, and as I was leaving to verify how clean we left it. We received our whole deposit bacj

Entry 11:
I’m in Churchill Downs right now and I think it’s fine. I haven’t had any issues and the apartments are nice enough especially for the price. From what I’ve heard the issues come after move out they will try to charge for anything they can. As long as you do a good job on move in and keep copies of everything you should be fine.

Entry 12:
How's the internet that they provide?

Entry 13:
it’s better than I expected. it’s by no means the fastest you can get but it worked pretty well and got me through the year with zoom classes.

Entry 14:
Check out Latah Apartments private message if u want a virtual tour lol

Entry 15:
I'm looking to get out of my lease at the Grove since I'm graduated if you want a nice place to live that's close to the Campus. If you're interested, hmu and we can discuss things.

Title:
Housing

Entry 2:
Hi everyone,
I was recently accepted into the College of Veterinary Medicine and I’m starting to look for housing. I would really appreciate recommendations on where to search for apartments close to the veterinary school.
Are there specific websites, rental agencies, or apartment complexes you would recommend? Also, are there any areas I should avoid?
Thank you in advance for your guidance!

Entry 3:
If graduate students are allowed, check the college-owned apartments Chinook Village, Columbia Village, Yakama Village. I know Yakama Village allows graduate students.
PS - Congratulations on your acceptance! That is a very big deal!

Entry 4:
I’ve got a place available! Two bedroom, 1.5 bath. The master bedroom is the open room (looking for a roommate). 0.8 miles away from the vet school. Washer/dryer, free parking. I walk to my office on campus every day, and can also walk to Main Street anytime I want, and there’s a park a minute’s walk from the apartment. I’m also a grad student and I’ve lived here 3 straight years and I like the place!
Rent for the whole unit is 1,220, so a 50/50 split would be 610 a month, but I’m very willing to lower that a bit if needed to fit within a budget. Feel free to reach out if you want photos/more info!

Entry 5:
Housing through WSU is always a great option! Cheap, no landlords to deal with, and fast maintenance!

Entry 6:
Pullman is a town owned and run by slumlords for the last 50+ years. Your best bet would be to try your luck with a private landlord. All the rental companies are awful.

Entry 7:
You should be getting access to the CVM housing packet soon. Craigslist is a good place to get a sense of what’s on offer and how much you need to budget.

Entry 8:
I'd check out the Grove, the Flats at Terre View, Aspen Heights, and any DABCO managed properties. Stay away from anything owned by DRA, Helenes Property Place, and Timberline on the Green. Muse on Main is also good but you have to pay extra for parking which is super expensive so not the place for you if you have a car. The college owned apartments are fine, but are older and you can get better amenities in a newer complex for the same price elsewhere. I've heard the annex and university crossing are good but are expensive!

Entry 9:
when i was at wsu back in 2016-2019, i lived in the wsu student housing - nez perce town homes for one year, & then lived at the flats at terre view for 2 years when they were brand new. loved both locations, & there are bus stops just about everywhere that you can ride free with your cougar card, & there is a bus stop by the vet hospital. i also worked at the vet hospital when i was a student, & you will probably want to take transit there if possible as parking options are not too great over there.

Entry 10:
Hi!! I also got into WSU Vet and I’ve been doing research/asking around. A lot of people said that DABCO is a good company to rent from and they have a few different apartment complexes to choose from. From what I can tell, they are all relatively similar. I’ve heard that other companies are predatory and to be careful of who you rent from.
WSU housing is inexpensive, but a lot of students have said to avoid. It’s better to rent off campus, not affiliated with the school.
If you have pets, I’ve found that a lot of places do not accept pets, so keep that in mind!

Title:
Housing Questions

Entry 2:
Hey all, I'm planning on attending WSU in the fall and I'm trying to get some living arrangements figured out early. There seems to be quite a few options and I'm seeing mixed opinions on all of them. Also a lot of my sources are several years old and I'm sure things are different. I'm curious where you guys are staying and your opinions about it. Open to either on-campus or off-campus options, not looking to spend more than $1k a month on rent + utilities + other fees if applicable

Entry 3:
Do not go to Timberline on the green. Are you a transfer or non-traditional student too? Otherwise, you have to live in the residence halls for the first year
Eta

Entry 4:
I'll be transferring as a sophomore, potentially junior. Depends on how much of my credits transfer

Entry 5:
Yes do not go to timberline
Dishwasher has been broken for 3 months
Only fixed bc I called weekly and nagged
They told my roommate she didn’t have a lease after she moved and signed w the old management company
Horrible place
I think same management also does aspen heights and college crest so would no recommend them for that reason
I’ve heard the chinook student apartments are decent!

Entry 6:
I’ve got a place for 610 a month a short walk from campus and I’m looking for a roommate, feel free to reach out to me if you’re interested and I can share more!

Entry 7:
Might take you up on that. I'll let you know for sure in March, waiting to hear back from a few other programs

Entry 8:
in 2016 i lived in the nez perce townhomes that are through wsu. i really enjoyed living there. there were bus stops close by, it was furnished, & the only bill i had to pay was power. rent was taken out of my loan disbursement before i got my leftover check, so i didnt have to worry about paying rent each month, & they covered wifi, water, sewer, & trash. the only downside was that there is no ac, & the insulation was horrible. i had ice build up on the inside of my windows, but that was also one of the worst winters pullman has had in recent years. they also have coin operated laundry in the mail room. after there in 2017, i moved into the flats at terre view, which they were brand new, & i lived there for 2 years. they were really nice, had an ac unit in the living room, laminate floors in the common spaces, & stayed really warm.

here we had to pay for wifi (get our own set up as well) and power. we had in unit laundry which was very convenient. they also have a small gym in the leasing center, but as a student you also have free access to the rec. both were great & i recommend both!!

Entry 9:
I live in a furnished unit in nez Perce right now, most of the units have been recently refurbished. The laundromat is free, all you need is your cougar card to access it. My friends live in a few of the other places in wsu and I feel like nez Perce is wayyy better than all the other places. You get two levels, a kitchen, lots of storage, and you only have to share your bathroom with your roommate. Also the only neighbors you have to deal with are on either side of you instead of above and below you like in a dorm or apartment complex, but generally everyone is pretty respectful of neighbors here.

Entry 10:
Just avoid some of the off campus slumlords like Apartment Rentals, formerly known as Apartments West. They have some pretty old rundown places and do the minimum in servicing. Been that way for 40 yrs.

Entry 11:
I'd recomend living off campus, better amenities and cheaper rent. I currently live in the Grove and love it ($600 a month covering all utilities and fully furnished for a 3 bedroom). I've also heard good things about Aspen Heights, University Crossing and the Retreat, but not sure how much/ what utilities they cover. If you split a house with a few friends, rent can be cheaper as well, but make sure to do research on the rental company (look at their google reviews and court records if applicable). Avoid DRA at all costs.

Entry 2:
There seems to be a major slumlord problem in Pullman. Currently I am residing in Aspen Heights, and they are terrible. Horrible communication, lots of BS fees, extremely high power bills (my last one was $136 when living alone), poor build quality (the walls are warped and the kitchen sink is held up with scrap wood), makes me interact with a rage-inducing company called PetScreening to have my cat, and obfuscates utility billing by making me use Conservice instead of paying Avista and the city directly. As they are currently owned by Cardinal Group Management, I am preemptively ruling out any complexes owned by them (including but not limited to: The Grove, Timberline on the green, and College crest). Ideally, any recommended complexes rent by apartment and not by room, and aren't on Greek row as I want to be able to sleep. Does anyone have any suggestions?

EDIT: I got my power bill for the month of december, and it's
$200 fucking dollars

Entry 3:
Not sure if either is still a thing, but when I was in Pullman Highland PM and Coug Housing were both straight up slum lords that never took care of anything. I think it’s more common than not in a majority of markets anymore.

Entry 4:
Currently live in a coug housing property: it's fine, seriously. They do throw on some extra fees that up the base rent a bit more than what they advertise, but it's nothing that isn't clearly stated in the contract you sign.
I've had emergency maintenance requests and non-emergency requests. They've responded to both promptly and addressed the issue the first time.
I even called about a bat that made it into the complex's hallway and they came and took care of it.

Entry 5:
Hey, well that’s good! They were super negligent around 15 years ago when I leased through them. They also tried to charge all kinds of predatory and exorbitant move out fees and I had to get a lawyer involved lmao. Someone at the time was clearly trying to take advantage of college kids’ finances.

Entry 6:
Don't know how long ago that was for you, but I worked in maintenance for Coug Housing for about a year and was pretty proud of how quickly we'd respond to things. But for like 15 years there was only two technicians TOTAL for the entire company and things have changed a bit.

Entry 7:
It was like 15 years ago so I’m glad to hear that things have turned around!

Entry 8:
Can recommend Highland Propert Mgmt. I've had no issues with them.

Entry 9:
Do not recommend DRA. We liked campus commons a lot more when we lived there

Entry 10:
DABCO is a decent leasing company. Never had any issues, good communication

Entry 11:
Yeah I’ve lived at 3 different dabco apartments. Schalimar, Birch Hills, and Aspen Village and haven’t had any issues with extra fees or not getting my deposit back.

Entry 12:
My daughter lives at Maple property. It’s been great so far, and she has already renewed her lease.

Entry 13:
On campus apartments are zero BS

Entry 14:
Providence Court was pretty good to me. Had one problem with a busted smoke alarm that wouldn’t stop going off and the repair guy was there within a half hour

Entry 15:
Issue with them is they'll try to charge you for regular maintenance after you leave, they tried to charge me the full cost to repaint the apartment/ redo the carpet despite little to no damage being present. Though I could've just been unlucky

Entry 16:
I think you were unlucky. Only fee I got was a $200 “cleaning fee” because my roommate left a box of trash in her room

Entry 17:
I don't know what your budget is like, but I lived in Whispering Hills and it was by far the best management I had throughout my six years in Pullman. Nice apartments, has a little dog park, receptive maintenance workers, and it's in a quiet community.

Entry 19:
I can whole heartedly recommend Pimlico, they're dabco owned, the 1 bedroom apartments are incredible for the price (945 a month + utilities), built in washer and dryer, my unit is absolutely perfect and so far it's the best apartment I've lived in. They also allow dogs and cats with a 150 per pet deposit, +25 to rent for dogs, +15 for cats which you handle through them during your application process, and they have covered parking available. It's also pretty close to campus. Electricity is through Avista and my bill is about 50 in the summer, closer to 100 in winter. Also pretty close to coug corner mart for those late night snack runs!

Entry 20:
Dabco is pretty good, and they rent by apartment! We had no issues getting our cat set up with them

Entry 21:
The Flats at Terre View are honestly great. Had a good time, especially coming from the Ruckus (new name now, can't remember it).

Entry 22:
I lived in three different dabco apartments and I honestly had a really good experience! Not super up-to-date but maintenance is quick and the price is reasonable!

Entry 23:
We had HORRIBLE communications with the Grove and decided not to go with them before moving here. Now we feel like we dodged a bullet. We absolutely love living here in birch hills. Its quiet on the edge of nothing, theyre super pet friendly, and our neighbors are very nice. Maintenance is quick and orderly( sometimes even same day). Its been so nice even compared to our previous landlords. We use avista for utilities too since that was a point you made. I HIGHLY recommend them compared to the horror stories I've heard and seen.

Entry 24:
My son had a decent experience with Cougar Ridge. They aren’t fancy and don’t have amenities like in apartment washers and dryers or a pool, but the rent seemed fair and he had no trouble with management or unfair fees when moving out. He shared it with his cousin for 2 years issue free.

Entry 25:
I had a bad experience there. Over priced, parking lot under permanent construction, multiple times the water got shut off for repairs and my apartment when I moved in was not cleaned well. Hair all over, dog kibble on the ground etc. I guess if you need a place pet friendly it's okay.

Entry 26:
The Annex is wonderful! Haven’t had any issues with them

Entry 27:
It's not cheap, but I have found University Crossing to be a pretty solid experience all things considered. It still uses PetScreening and Conservice (although you don't pay through them, just through the apartment portal), but utilities have been pretty fair, everything is of decent quality, and the front desk and maintenance have been nothing but quick and helpful.

Entry 28:
I lived in chief Joseph for 4 years. Decent apartments, owned by the university. No AC though. But, you can always by a portable one.

Entry 29:
I love willow place they’re super super nice people and super fast for any issues it’s not the newest but the rent is good and I’ve had no complaints the last 3yrs

Entry 30:
Timberline on The Green (formerly The Ruckus) was chaos. I liked “living” when I mean by “living” I mean literally sleeping on the bed. Maintenance took forever to fix any issues, leasing staff never answers my email or phone call. They also entered my unit without giving me a notice. It was nightmare. I wouldn’t recommend living there overall.

Entry 31:
I lived at pimlico and thought it was a really comfortable place

Entry 32:
I've lived in an Apartment Rentals Inc property for the last 8 years. Rent is reasonable, utilities are paid directly to Avista and your chosen ISP and maintenance is responsive to problems.

Entry 33:
hey, i’m looking at living there, have you ever had any problems? it seems a little sketchy just worried about extra charges when the lease is up

Entry 34:
I can't speak to any charges when moving out I have been renting with them my whole time in Pullman. I can say that in my two experiences transferring units I had no extra fees or other problems.

Entry 35:
Lots of new properties out on the north east edge of campus….They are looking kind of vacant

Entry 36:
personally have had an amazing time living at the retreat

Entry 37:
I was at cougar place, right by Valhalla. Enjoyed it and coug housing was generally helpful

Entry 38:
My son lives there his around year same apt just new roommates for him. They have been great and super helpful with a psycho roommate they had to deal with and eventually evict

Entry 39:
Cougar Crest was the cream of the crop when I lived there.

Entry 41:
I also reside in aspen right now and so far my roommate and I looked at the retreat and the hills, and signed with the hills. Seems affordable for all the amenities you get

Entry 42:
I’m at timberline now in a one bedroom. Rent isn’t awful imo for the space. I think I am paying like 1100 for a one bedroom. The fees are a little suspicious here and there, like my power bill for example….is somehow over a hundred bucks….and I live alone. The front office is and will always be terrible. I signed a lease in June with some summer package, like three months paid for free or whatever it was…I checked in with them multiple times like “hey y’all are tracking that? Ok how are you, is it in the lease?” And they always said yeah you’re good. Three months later they were asking for rent….had to get some strongly yet tactfully pissed emails into them….and all it took was the one difficult person to take a day off and it was fixed by someone else whom I’m sure didn’t want to argue any further.

Side note: asked the office to remove a bunch of maintenance supplies out from the front of my door for prolly two months before they actually did anything. I just went in one day and was like, “yo I’m just gonna throw this all away at this point if it isn’t moved by tomorrow” and that seemed to work. I was in the military before this so I was just finally happy to have my own place, but I did seriously consider moving to Moscow, and paying just a little extra so that I wouldn’t be in a “college apartment” anymore…but the rent here isn’t awful enough to complain too much, its warm in the winter, and shit.

Title:

Entry 3:
WSU/pullman rent is greatly affordable, while I’m sure it has gone up the last two years I’ve been away it’s still in this 400-600$ range for many places and food prices are also a bit lower than the other side of the state. 1k is more than enough just don’t eat out too much.

Title:
Moving off campus before entering housing contract

Entry 2:
Hello, I am a returning student who lived in Mceachern last year and was assigned back to the same room a few months ago. I was originally planning on being in the dorms again but recently decided to live in an apartment off campus. I have not entered the housing contract for this upcoming year, however in WSU's policies it talks about being charged for canceling prior to check in. I was wondering if anyone knows whether I will still be okay to tell them I am not moving back on campus this year or if they will still find a way to charge me a cancellation fee? Even though I haven't entered the contract, I get stressed when it comes to WSU housing as I feel their policies are not always easy to follow and don't want to end up with a large fee.

Entry 4:
You need to cancel your application before you move to off campus apartment. You may not get full refund as I think deadline to cancel application with refund has closed. If you have any questions or concerns, you can contact housing main office at
housing@wsu.edu

Entry 5:
They don’t ask for a deposit until late July so they only have to cancel but the later they chose to cancel the more they accumulate late penalties.

Entry 6:
So from my understanding with the housing contract, when you chose to be placed in your same dorm room again (i was also in McEachern and chose the same option) it basically renews your contract for the fall and the deadline to cancel your contract without accruing fees is before June 15th. After the 15th it’s a $200 fee, then $400, then $750. So the longer you wait to cancel the more you accrue in late penalties.

Entry 7:
I would call them today to talk to them to avoid getting charged a cancellation fee

Daughter moved out of Cougar Ridge to Dabco. Although there is no AC, the apt is much nicer. It has an in unit washer/dryer. Pullman does not get humid but fans help. Not Texas hot. Kid has a portable unit for bedroom.

Entry 11:
If your a student, they’re all gonna try to rip you off. Avoid Heritage Realty. I’m not a student and I’m still getting scammed by these clowns.

Entry 12:
It’s a little further from campus and I’m not sure of your budget/lifestyle but The Residence at Whispering Hills. Here’s all their
amenities

Entry 13:
I used to live under slocum apartments. He’s a nice guy, but his properties aren’t that great and are usually located near Greek row/very loud and busy places near campus. I would recommend to go with something further away from campus. We live in birch hills. It’s “nice” apartments. The neighbors are quiet, friendly, and the crowd is mostly matured adults with full time jobs vs young college kids. Management is ass. All dabco properties are kind of a nightmare in terms of management but they own most of the apartmen complexes in this area. Dabco managed properties are super easy to get accepted into, so that’s a plus. I’d recommend something on this side of town, as it stays quiet and a bit more secluded.

Entry 14:
Hi, I’m also starting PhD in the fall. How do you find roommates? It’s really expensive that the type of 1b1b.

Entry 16:
I lived in Cougar Ridge, Providence Court, and Maple Valley apartments and had friends in the Grove. Of those, I’d definitely recommend the Dabco properties - the Providence Court location was my favorite for the proximity to campus and the Grand/Stadium Way corner. Generally, the units are well maintained, if you need maintenance for any reason they show up fairly quickly, and they don’t nickel and dime you too much on move out. The cougar ridge unit I moved into with a roommate was an absolute nightmare. The carpet smelled like pee - which they refused to do anything about - the unit was dirty upon move in, and it also lacked a dishwasher even though we were told it was going to have one when we signed our lease. It also was significantly more expensive than other comparable units in Pullman (no clue why my roommate and I decided we wanted to spring for it).

As far as the grove goes, my friend was happy with it, but the rent was too expensive for me to think it was worth it. The nice thing about Pullman is almost everyone is living in the same (ish) base conditions. I think it’s part of what brings people together. Might as well enjoy the cheapest rent you can get and not worry about paying a large amount more just so you don’t have to take a 30sec walk outside for laundry. All that being said, everyone has different priorities! Best of luck to you, and know that you’ll have a great time regardless of where you live because you’re in Pullman! Go Cougs!!
